sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Brian reports feeling out of sorts lately, with fatigue, lightheadedness, and decreased energy.
History of Present Illness
Brian reports feeling out of sorts for approximately five weeks, starting around Labor Day. He attributes this to increased activity related to home construction, including frequent meals eaten out due to difficulty cooking. He denies fever or chills. He reports slight cramps that resolve within an hour. He has been managing his heart failure with dietary changes and reports being relatively consistent with medication adherence, including monitoring his blood pressure with a home cuff. He reports no nausea or vomiting. He has been taking his medications as prescribed and monitoring his blood pressure regularly.
Findings
Physical exam reveals jugular venous distention to 8 cm, a three out of six systolic ejection murmur, and 1+ pitting edema bilaterally in the lower extremities. Vital signs are stable with blood pressure 132/82 mmHg and heart rate 72 bpm. The chest x-ray demonstrates mild pulmonary edema, and the echocardiogram shows a reduced ejection fraction of 45% and mitral regurgitation.
Assessment
Brian presents with congestive heart failure, likely exacerbated by recent strenuous activity and dietary changes. He also has hypertension and mild pulmonary edema.
Plan

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: Increase lasix to 80 mg daily. Weigh himself daily and contact the provider if he gains more than 2 pounds in 24 hours. Order an echocardiogram to assess cardiac function. Continue lisinopril 20 mg daily. Schedule a follow-up appointment in four weeks to assess response to treatment and monitor symptoms. Finalize the note.
